title: Help command and usage message

story.i_want_to: see a help message that explains all available commands and their usage
story.so_that: I can quickly learn or remind myself how to use the app

rationale: Even simple CLI tools benefit from built-in help. Users shouldn't have to search docs or source code to remember the syntax for a command they use infrequently.

acceptance_criteria.description: Running `todo help` displays a usage message listing all commands with descriptions and argument syntax.
The help message includes: add, list, done, delete, edit, help commands with their argument syntax.
The help message shows example usage for add, done, delete, and edit commands.
Running `todo` with no arguments also shows the help message (same as `todo help`), printing to stdout with exit code 0.
Running `todo --help` or `todo -h` produces the same help output with exit code 0.
Running `todo --version` prints the app version (e.g., 'todo v0.1.0') and exits with code 0.
The help text includes the list filter options: '--done', '--pending', '--all'.
Running `todo help <command>` shows detailed usage and examples for that specific command. Running `todo help <unknown>` prints 'Unknown command: {command}. Run `todo help` for available commands.'

test_cases.name: No arguments shows same help as help command
expected_results: Both commands produce identical stdout output

test_cases.name: --help and -h flags work
expected_results: Both produce the same help output

test_cases.name: Help includes examples for ID-based commands
expected_results: stdout contains at least one example with a numeric ID (e.g., 'todo done 3')
stdout contains at least one add example (e.g., 'todo add "Buy groceries"')

test_cases.name: Per-command help works
steps: Run `python todo.py help add` with HOME=tmp_dir
expected_results: stdout contains detailed usage for the add command
stdout contains at least one example

test_cases.name: Help text stays in sync with registered commands
preconditions: Access to the command dispatch table and help text
steps: Parse the help text output and extract all command names mentioned
Compare against the set of registered command handler keys
expected_results: Every registered command appears in the help text
No help text references a command that doesn't exist

technical_notes: Define help text as a multi-line string constant
Match the CLI interface spec from the PRD exactly
Reuse the same function for 'no args', 'help', '--help', and '-h' paths
Include per-command help dicts with usage, description, and examples
Add a sync test to prevent help drift as commands are added
Define VERSION = '0.1.0' constant, used by both --version and help header
